private.name: Apex Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.3B

data.narrative:
Gilmour Space Technologies is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company that has raised approximately A$100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: Sierra Space ($8.0B, Series C), Apex Space ($2.3B, Series D), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B, Series C), and Skyryse ($1.1B, Series C).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a valuation of $1.9B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $8.0B across the comparison set. This method anchors the analysis given the availability of direct stage and sector comparables. A public-comp anchoring approach using six Space & Aerospace public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 25.8x is not applicable, as Gilmour's revenue is not on file—a common constraint for early-stage private companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.6B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.9B. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peers (driven largely by Sierra Space's $8.0B outlier valuation), the absence of revenue-based public comparables, and limited visibility into Gilmour's operational metrics relative to stage peers. Additional revenue disclosure or traction data would strengthen conviction in this range.
